relationView.noAnalysis: No analysis is available for the selected entity yet.
relationView.noAttackPath: No attack path available for the selected entity
relationView.noDirectRootRelation: There is no direct edge to the root node. See the full relation list below.
relationView.indirectPathSummary: This node can reach the root node through a {count}-hop path:
relationView.pathRoleThreatActorDesc: This node sits at the start of the attack path and represents the actor driving or organizing the attack.
relationView.pathRoleAttackToolDesc: This node sits in the middle of the attack path and represents the tool or capability being built, held, or used.
relationView.pathRoleRiskDesc: This node sits in the outcome part of the attack path and represents the risk being directly caused or indirectly driven.
relationView.pathRoleAvoidanceDesc: This node sits at the closing part of the attack path and represents a mitigation or avoidance measure.
relationView.hiddenRelationCount: {count} more relations are hidden. Click to view all.
relationView.collapseRelationCount: Collapse all relations and show the first 15 only.
relationView.noFilteredAttackPaths: No attack paths match the current filter combination.
relationView.hiddenAttackPathCount: {count} more paths are hidden. Click to view all.
relationView.collapseAttackPathCount: Collapse attack paths and show the first 3 only.

nodeAnalysis.risk: Risk "{title}" has {relations} relations, connecting {attackTools} attack tools, {threatActors} threat actors, and {avoidances} avoidance measures.
nodeAnalysis.avoidance: Avoidance "{title}" has {relations} relations, covering {risks} risks and connecting {attackTools} attack tools.
nodeAnalysis.attack-tool: Attack tool "{title}" has {relations} relations, connecting {risks} risks, {threatActors} threat actors, and {avoidances} avoidance measures.
nodeAnalysis.threat-actor: Threat actor "{title}" has {relations} relations, connecting {attackTools} attack tools and {risks} risks.
nodeAnalysis.term: Term "{title}" has {relations} relations, connecting {risks} risks, {avoidances} avoidance measures, {attackTools} attack tools, and {threatActors} threat actors.

nodeAnalysisNotice.highConnectivity: This node has many connections and is useful as a relation analysis entry point.
nodeAnalysisNotice.lowConnectivity: This node has few relations and is currently more useful for local inspection.
nodeAnalysisNotice.missingAvoidance: This risk has no explicit avoidance coverage yet and needs follow-up.
nodeAnalysisNotice.missingRiskLink: This attack tool has no explicit risk link yet and should be reviewed.
nodeAnalysisNotice.rootPath: This node can return to the root through a {count}-hop path.

relationView.businessSceneImpactSummary: Covers {businessScenes} business scenes across {dimensions} business dimensions.
relationView.businessSceneImpactSummaryEmpty: No business-scene impact can be mapped for this node yet.
relationView.businessSceneImpactNoticeEmpty: This node does not hit a business-scene mapping yet. Complete the scene definitions if this is expected to be covered.

nodeCoverageTitle.attackTool: Risk and avoidance coverage for attack tool "{title}"

nodeCoverageSummary.risk: {total} candidate avoidances: {direct} from the risk, {tool} from related tools, {overlap} shared by both.
nodeCoverageSummary.avoidance: This avoidance covers {risks} risks and {tools} attack tools, and traces back to {actors} related threat actors.
nodeCoverageSummary.attackTool: This tool is associated with {risks} risks and already has {avoidances} avoidances that may limit or mitigate it.
nodeCoverageSummary.threatActor: This actor is associated with {tools} attack tools and {risks} risks, which helps bound the capability scope.

relationView.nodeCoverageMetaDirect: From the risk's own avoidance list
relationView.nodeCoverageMetaBoth: Referenced by both risk and related tools
relationView.nodeCoverageMetaRisk: From risk avoidance or risk-linked fields

nodeCoverageNotice.risk: This risk has no avoidance coverage yet and should be prioritized for mitigation work.
nodeCoverageNotice.avoidance: This avoidance currently does not cover a clear risk or tool and should be reviewed.
nodeCoverageNotice.attackTool: This attack tool is not linked to a clear risk yet and should be completed.
nodeCoverageNotice.threatActor: This threat actor is not linked to tools or risks yet and should be reviewed.

relationView.hiddenCoverageItemCount: {count} more coverage items are hidden. Click to view all.
relationView.collapseCoverageItemCount: Collapse coverage and show the first 8 only.

relationExplanation.riskAvoidance: This relation means the risk explicitly references an avoidance measure that can mitigate or reduce it.
relationExplanation.toolAvoidance: This relation means the attack tool explicitly references an avoidance measure that can limit or counter it.
relationExplanation.avoidance: This relation carries a defensive or mitigation meaning between the current entity and an avoidance measure.
relationExplanation.directCauseRisk: This relation means the source entity is marked as directly causing the target risk.
relationExplanation.indirectSupportRisk: This relation means the source entity indirectly supports or relates to the target risk.
relationExplanation.buildAttackTool: This relation means the threat actor can build, maintain, or develop the attack tool.
relationExplanation.useAttackTool: This relation means the threat actor uses the attack tool in related activity.
relationExplanation.causeRisk: This relation summarizes direct and indirect risk-causation fields for quick exposure review.
relationExplanation.relatedTerm: This relation comes from term-related entity fields and explains terminology context.
relationExplanation.subEntity: This relation comes from child entity IDs and explains parent-child ownership.
relationExplanation.attackToolMaker: This relation traces the attack tool back to its builder.
relationExplanation.review: This relation does not yet have a specific explanation template; review it with the source fields.

relationImpact.avoidance: Analysis value: candidate defensive coverage or blocking point.
relationImpact.directCauseRisk: Analysis value: prioritize this direct source-to-risk chain.
relationImpact.indirectSupportRisk: Analysis value: useful context, but it should not be treated as direct causality.
relationImpact.threatActorTool: Analysis value: helps explain actor capability and operating style.
relationImpact.causeRisk: Analysis value: useful for finding risk sources, but direct and indirect fields should be distinguished.
relationImpact.relatedTerm: Analysis value: improves semantic search and term explanation.
relationImpact.subEntity: Analysis value: expands the detailed scenarios or techniques under a parent entity.
relationImpact.review: Analysis value: this relation needs further human or AI judgment.

relationView.attackPathExplanationSummary: This path contains {count} reasoning steps, with {risk} as the core risk.
relationView.attackPathGroupedExplanationSummary: Merged {pathCount} similar paths across {actorCount} threat actors, with {risk} as the core risk.

attackPathFinding.toolRisk: {actorCount} threat actors connect to "{risk}" through "{tool}". The tool-to-risk relation is "{relation}", and the current mitigation candidate is "{avoidance}".
attackPathFinding.singleActorToolRisk: "{actor}" connects to "{risk}" through "{tool}". The tool-to-risk relation is "{relation}", and the current mitigation candidate is "{avoidance}".
attackPathFinding.actorRisk: {actorCount} threat actors connect directly to "{risk}". The current mitigation candidate is "{avoidance}".
attackPathFinding.singleActorRisk: "{actor}" connects directly to "{risk}". The current mitigation candidate is "{avoidance}".

attackPathRecommendedAction.toolRisk: Prioritize traces of "{tool}", then validate whether "{avoidance}" sufficiently covers the trigger conditions of "{risk}".
attackPathRecommendedAction.actorRisk: Prioritize the evidence chain between the threat actor and "{risk}", then validate whether "{avoidance}" is an effective mitigation.

attackPathIntent.actorToTool: Attack intent: the threat actor gains executable capability through tool use or development.
attackPathIntent.toolToRisk: Attack intent: the attack tool drives the risk, either directly or indirectly.
attackPathIntent.actorToRisk: Attack intent: the threat actor has a direct or indirect association with the risk.
attackPathIntent.riskToAvoidance: Attack intent: after the risk node, mitigation depends on an avoidance measure.

attackPathDefense.actorToTool: Defensive meaning: monitor actor tooling, usage traces, and capability-building signals.
attackPathDefense.toolToRisk: Defensive meaning: build detection or controls around tool behavior, triggers, and risk outcomes.
attackPathDefense.actorToRisk: Defensive meaning: prioritize reviewing the evidence chain between the actor and the risk.
attackPathDefense.riskToAvoidance: Defensive meaning: this avoidance measure is the direct mitigation candidate for the path.

relationExplanation.riskAvoidance: The risk entity directly lists this avoidance measure.
relationExplanation.toolAvoidance: The attack-tool entity directly lists this avoidance measure.
relationExplanation.avoidance: This relation indicates a mitigation or avoidance path.
relationExplanation.directCauseRisk: The source entity lists this risk as a directly caused outcome.
relationExplanation.indirectSupportRisk: The source entity lists this risk as an indirectly supported outcome.
relationExplanation.buildAttackTool: The threat-actor entity lists this attack tool as a built capability.
relationExplanation.useAttackTool: The threat-actor entity lists this attack tool as a used capability.
relationExplanation.causeRisk: This relation aggregates direct-cause and indirect-support risk fields.
relationExplanation.relatedTerm: The term entity lists this node as related knowledge.
relationExplanation.subEntity: This relation comes from the main/sub entity ID hierarchy.
relationExplanation.attackToolMaker: This relation comes from the threat actor's built-tool field.
relationExplanation.review: This relation lacks a classified explanation and should be reviewed.

relationImpact.avoidance: Use this to judge mitigation coverage for a risk or tool.
relationImpact.directCauseRisk: Treat this as a high-confidence attack outcome link.
relationImpact.indirectSupportRisk: Use this as an upstream or downstream risk-analysis clue.
relationImpact.threatActorTool: Use this to identify actor capabilities and tool usage.
relationImpact.causeRisk: Check the source fields to distinguish direct and indirect relations.
relationImpact.relatedTerm: This explains terminology context and is not attack causality.
relationImpact.subEntity: This expands finer-grained entities and is not a standalone attack step.
relationImpact.review: Add source or relationship semantics during data maintenance.

relationView.filteredSimplePathCount: {count} attack paths match the current filters.
relationView.pathDetailSummary: Shows one attack chain under the current filters and the evidence behind each segment.
relationView.pathReasonBuildAndUseTool: The threat actor is recorded as both building and using this attack tool.
relationView.pathReasonBuildTool: The threat actor is recorded as building this attack tool.
relationView.pathReasonUseTool: The threat actor is recorded as using this attack tool.
relationView.pathReasonDirectRisk: The source entity is recorded as directly causing this risk.
relationView.pathReasonIndirectRisk: The source entity is recorded as indirectly supporting or driving this risk.
relationView.pathReasonRiskAvoidance: This avoidance comes from the risk's own avoidance list.
relationView.pathReasonRelated: This path segment comes from entity relationship fields.
relationView.coverageModeSummary: {total} avoidances: {direct} from the risk, {tool} from related tools, {overlap} shared.

search.placeholder: Search risks, avoidances, attack tools, slang terms...
